title: Listening - Part 4: Experiences with House-Sharing
description: You are going to read a magazine article about an intellectual process known as critical thinking. For questions 31 – 36, choose the answer (A, B, C or D) which you think fits best according to the text. Mark your answers on the separate answer sheet.
topic: five people discussing their experiences of sharing a home with others in adulthood

content.audio_script: Speaker 1: I’d lived alone for years, but [21]I decided to find a house-share primarily because the rising rent in the city was becoming completely unaffordable on my salary[/21]. It was a financial necessity. [26]The most unexpected benefit has been the dramatic improvement in my cooking skills[/26]. One of my housemates is a professional chef, and he’s taught me so much. I now actually enjoy preparing meals, which has saved me even more money on takeaways. || Speaker 2: We’re a group of four friends, and [22]we chose to buy a large house together because we wanted to have a sense of community that you just don't get in a solo flat[/22]. We wanted a support system. However, [27]the biggest challenge has been the constant negotiations over cleaning and domestic chores[/27]. Even among friends, everyone has a different standard of what 'tidy' looks like. We’ve had to implement a very strict rota just to avoid constant arguments. || Speaker 3: I’m a freelance writer, and [23]I moved into a large shared house because I was finding the isolation of working from home to be detrimental to my mental health[/23]. I needed people around. [28]What I didn't expect was how much my creative focus would actually improve[/28]. Having other people in the house who are also working makes me feel much more disciplined. I don't get distracted by the TV or laundry because I want to set a good example of productivity. || Speaker 4: I’m in my sixties and [24]I joined a multi-generational house-share because I wanted to stay connected to younger people and feel less 'retired'[/24]. I didn't want to live in a bubble. [29]The most rewarding outcome has been the massive boost in my own tech-savviness[/29]. My younger housemates have taught me how to use all sorts of apps and gadgets that I would have been too intimidated to try alone. I feel much more part of the modern world now. || Speaker 5: I’d recently gone through a divorce and [25]I moved into a shared house because I was terrified of coming home to an empty building every night[/25]. I needed the security of other people. [30]The most significant result for me has been the wonderful and diverse social circle I’ve built[/30]. I’ve met people from different cultures and professions that I never would have crossed paths with otherwise. It’s broadened my horizons and helped me heal in a way that living alone never could have.

task1.title: Task One
task1.instruction: For questions 21–25, choose from the list (A–H) what primarily motivated each speaker to share a home.

questions.speaker: Speaker 1
questions.explanation: Speaker 1 needed to share because the rising rent was unaffordable on her salary.

questions.speaker: Speaker 2
questions.explanation: Speaker 2 wanted a sense of community and a support system.

questions.speaker: Speaker 3
questions.explanation: Speaker 3 found the isolation of working from home affected her mental health.

questions.speaker: Speaker 4
questions.explanation: Speaker 4 wanted to stay connected to younger generations and feel less retired.

questions.speaker: Speaker 5
questions.explanation: Speaker 5 wanted to avoid the loneliness of an empty home after her divorce.

options.A: to reduce their monthly living costs
options.B: to be closer to a new job
options.C: to avoid the loneliness of living alone
options.D: to help a friend in financial trouble
options.E: to live in a more prestigious area
options.G: to create a deliberate support network

task2.title: Task Two
task2.instruction: For questions 26–30, choose from the list (A–H) what each speaker mentions as an outcome of their experience.

questions.speaker: Speaker 1
questions.explanation: Speaker 1 mentions that her cooking skills improved thanks to a housemate.

questions.speaker: Speaker 2
questions.explanation: Speaker 2 mentions the challenge of constant negotiations over cleaning and chores.

questions.speaker: Speaker 3
questions.explanation: Speaker 3 found that her creative focus and discipline improved.

questions.speaker: Speaker 4
questions.explanation: Speaker 4 notes a boost in her tech-savviness thanks to younger housemates.

questions.speaker: Speaker 5
questions.explanation: Speaker 5 mentions building a diverse social circle and broadening her horizons.

options.A: the discovery of a lucrative new hobby
options.B: the acquisition of a practical life skill
options.C: the realization that they prefer living alone
options.D: an improvement in their financial management
options.E: a surprising boost in their work productivity
options.F: the challenge of managing domestic expectations
options.G: an increase in their digital competence
options.H: the development of a wider social network
